description: Part of a letter, probably from the first half of the 11th century, possibly written by Yeshuʿa b. Yosef ha-Kohen, on behalf of the Jerusalemite and Babylonian Rabbanite communities in Alexandria, to the leader of the Jewish community in Fusṭāṭ (name not preserved but titled ‘the distinguished scholar’), perhaps Elḥanan b. Shemarya, regarding the plight of five Jewish male youths who were cruelly held captive and, having been ransomed for 100 dinars, subsequently fell ill. The ransom had been raised at great cost to every member of the Alexandrian Jewish community. (Information from CUDL.)
description_he: חלק ממכתב, כנראה מהמחצית הראשונה של המאה ה-11. בשם קהילות רבניות ירושלמיות ובבליות באלכסנדריה, לראש הקהילה בפוסטאט. בנוגע לחמישה בחורים יהודים שנלקחו בשבי ונפדו ב-100 דינרים אחרי שחלו
